title: Baked Zucchini Chickpea Curry
image_alt: Baked Zucchini Chickpea Curry in a bowl with rice
modifications: Omitted hot sauce and sambal oelek — substituted Sweet Chili Sauce instead
Added broccoli and cauliflower in similar quantities to the zucchini
Used garlic powder instead of fresh garlic / ginger garlic paste

items.name: Sweet Chili Sauce ✱ replaces hot sauce + sambal oelek
garlic powder ✱ replaces ginger garlic paste
canned tomato puree (or 2 Tbsp tomato paste)

steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (205°C). Grease a 9×12" baking dish with oil. Add onion, zucchini, broccoli, cauliflower, and sweet chili sauce, tossing well.
Mix all spices in a small bowl and sprinkle over the veggie mixture. Spread evenly in the pan. Bake for 10–14 minutes until onions are translucent.
Remove from oven. Mix in garlic powder and tomato puree. Add coconut milk, chickpeas, and spinach. Stir well and spread evenly.
Return to oven and bake 20–30 minutes, until sauce is bubbling and slightly thickened. If you need it thicker, stir in a few tablespoons of non-dairy yogurt while still hot.
Garnish with cilantro and lime juice. Serve with rice, naan, flatbread, or garlic bread.
